user_query:
We’re gearing up for a Q2 push in the AI-powered RFP response software market and need a compact, actionable competitive intelligence pack. Please read the files under input/ and produce a set of structured outputs under output/ as follows:

1) Market map (output/market_map.yaml)
- Use the 4-tier schema (direct, adjacent, indirect, emerging) and include at least 8 competitors with the template fields (date, tier, website, employee_trend, primary_market, positioning, strengths, weaknesses, threat_level, notes). Use ISO 8601 dates.

2) Feature matrix (output/feature_matrix.yaml)
- Include last_updated (ISO date), at least 3 categories, and at least 10 total features. Add a top-level competitor_keys list of the short keys you’ll use (e.g., ["us","compA","compB","compC"]). For every feature, include ratings for us and at least 3 competitors and a 1–5 weight. Note important notes/assumptions.

3) Pricing intel (output/pricing_intel.yaml)
- Include our pricing as "Us" and at least 3 competitors, each with model, entry_price, mid_price or enterprise_price, free_tier, annual_discount, contract_required, implementation_fee, hidden_costs, and a brief total_cost_notes field explaining TCO considerations.

4) Sales battlecards (output/battlecards/)
- Generate one Markdown file per direct competitor identified (minimum 2 files). File naming: output/battlecards/<competitor_slug>.md. Each must contain the sections from the template: title line beginning with "# 🏆 Battlecard: Us vs ", Quick Stats table, Their Pitch, Why Customers Choose Us Over Them, Why Customers Choose Them Over Us (with counters), Landmines to Plant, Objection Handling, Trap Plays, Win Stories, Recent Intel, with a Last Updated date.

5) Win/loss roll-up (output/win_loss/summary.json and output/win_loss/quarterly_review.md)
- Compute a by_competitor roll-up with wins, losses, and win_rate per competitor, plus an overall section with wins, losses, win_rate. Include top_win_reasons and top_loss_reasons arrays (strings, ranked by frequency). In the Markdown, include a "Win Rate by Competitor" table and short lists of top win/loss reasons.

6) Weekly monitoring brief (output/monitoring/weekly_brief.md)
- Use the brief template with sections: Critical (🔴), Notable (🟡), Informational (🟢), and a short Win/Loss This Week section.

Important:
- Read input/brief.md for our positioning/ICP and input/competitors.csv for seed names. Use input/win_loss.jsonl to compute the roll-ups. If you have to assume anything (e.g., pricing details not provided), mark it clearly as assumptions in notes fields or an Assumptions section.
- Keep all paths relative and write only to the specified output/ locations.
- Use ethical public-source framing and do not invent unverifiable customer quotes; clearly label examples or placeholders where needed.
- If anything is unclear, ask clarifying questions before proceeding.
